steps.title: Keep subject with verb
steps.content: The subject is "Puerto Rico's museums," and the verb is "showcase." Standard English does not place punctuation between a subject and its verb when no interrupting phrase is present.

steps.title: Reject separating punctuation
steps.content: B inserts a dash between the subject and verb, C inserts a comma, and D inserts a colon. Each incorrectly breaks the subject-verb connection. The answer is A.
